summary: A high‑energy Denver FOB audience tape that captures standout performances of Peggy‑O, Little Red Rooster and Terrapin Station, despite occasional mix quirks and chatter.
review: The recording is a solid front‑of‑house (FOB) audience capture that most reviewers describe as clear with clean tape changes, though a few note boxy tones and mic‑adjustment chatter. Musically the show shines – Jerry’s vocals on Peggy‑O are praised, Little Red Rooster and Terrapin Station receive glowing mentions, and the second set is repeatedly called “whipping ass.” A handful of listeners point out mix imbalances (Vince too forward, Bob buried) and a negative reaction to Corrina.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 4‑5 stars praising the performance and noting good audience recording quality; only a few low‑star comments cite minor mix issues, leading to an overall strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear FOB tape with clean tape changes and good ambience; occasional mic readjustments cause chatter, some beeping sounds, a boxy tonal quality, and imbalance (Vince loud, Bob recessed).

show_quality.setlist_flow: Both sets are described as solid and energetic, with the second set especially praised for nonstop momentum; the blizzard/storm backdrop adds to the memorable atmosphere.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals in rare form on Peggy‑O; generally feeling good throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: One reviewer notes Bob (Weir) is too far back in the mix.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince Welnick is described as overly prominent in at least one mix comment.
